title: Low-water forecasting for Rhine-corridor logistics
summary: CEMS discharge forecasts and CLMS surface-water products supported load planning and low-water early warning on a major river corridor.
narrative:
## Challenge
Low water during drought forces reduced barge loading or halts navigation, with major logistics impacts that are hard to anticipate days in advance.

## Approach
CEMS flood-awareness discharge forecasts (EFAS) and CLMS water-and-wetness products were combined to anticipate water levels and surface-water extent along the corridor, supporting navigability and load decisions.

## Results
The operator improved load planning and gained earlier warning of low-water periods, reducing last-minute disruption and improving fleet utilisation.
